title: China Remains Uniquely Positioned To Carry Out Stimulus
section_path: China Remains Uniquely Positioned To Carry Out Stimulus
section_path_text: China Remains Uniquely Positioned To Carry Out Stimulus
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly China Report, inflation in China most recently came in at 0.5% in January. This is the highest inflation seen in China since January, but it is still relatively low compared to other major economies. Inflation in the United States, for example, came in at 3% in January.
